title: A conservative update preserves total mass when boundary fluxes agree

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the two boundary fluxes flux(n) and flux(0) are equal, then the conservative update preserves the total over the first n cells.
assumptions: u and flux are real-valued functions on natural-number grid indices
informal.expected_conclusion: The total after the conservative update equals the original total.

declarations.name: totalOn_conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n - scale * (flux n - flux 0)

declarations.name: totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : flux n = flux 0) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Translate the boundary-flux total-preservation theorem into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : flux n = flux 0) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Given totalOn_conservativeUpdate and the boundary equality assumption, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by rewriting with totalOn_conservativeUpdate and the boundary equality, then closing with ring.
